patch:
diff --git a/kombu/utils/functional.py b/kombu/utils/functional.py
index 29c3d96dc7..400fe2dd84 100644
--- a/kombu/utils/functional.py
+++ b/kombu/utils/functional.py
@@ -31,8 +31,9 @@ def __call__(self):
         try:
             return self.__value__
         except AttributeError:
-            value = self.__value__ = self.__contract__()
-            return value
+            pass
+        value = self.__value__ = self.__contract__()
+        return value
 
     def __repr__(self):
         try:
test_patch:
diff --git a/t/unit/utils/test_functional.py b/t/unit/utils/test_functional.py
index 8acdffa874..4061b1b32e 100644
--- a/t/unit/utils/test_functional.py
+++ b/t/unit/utils/test_functional.py
@@ -20,6 +20,19 @@ def test_repr(self):
         assert 'promise' in repr(ChannelPromise(obj))
         obj.assert_not_called()
 
+    def test_failing_contract(self):
+        def failing():
+            raise RuntimeError("failure for test")
+
+        p = ChannelPromise(failing)
+
+        with pytest.raises(RuntimeError, match="failure for test") as excinfo:
+            p()
+
+        # ensure the exception is not chained with internal ChannelPromise errors
+        assert excinfo.value.__context__ is None
+        assert excinfo.value.__cause__ is None
+
 
 class test_shufflecycle:
problem_statement:
Weird ChannelPromise error in the traceback when connection fails
Packages versions

```bash
kombu                     5.6.2
celery                    5.6.2
redis                     7.1.1
hiredis                   3.3.0
```

Reproduce with this command

```bash
python -c "from celery.execute import send_task;send_task('package.module.mytask')"
```

The start of the traceback puts people on the wrong track because to error is that we did not define any celery configuration and the connection to the default RabbitMQ local port fails, not some random thing in `ChannelPromise`

```bash
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/kombu/utils/functional.py", line 32, in __call__
    return self.__value__
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
AttributeError: 'ChannelPromise' object has no attribute '__value__'. Did you mean: '__call__'?

During handling of the above exception, another exception occurred:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/kombu/connection.py", line 474, in _reraise_as_library_errors
    yield
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/kombu/connection.py", line 461, in _ensure_connection
    return retry_over_time(
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/kombu/utils/functional.py", line 318, in retry_over_time
    return fun(*args, **kwargs)
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/kombu/connection.py", line 941, in _connection_factory
    self._connection = self._establish_connection()
                       ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/kombu/connection.py", line 863, in _establish_connection
    conn = self.transport.establish_connection()
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/kombu/transport/pyamqp.py", line 203, in establish_connection
    conn.connect()
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/amqp/connection.py", line 324, in connect
    self.transport.connect()
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/amqp/transport.py", line 132, in connect
    self._connect(self.host, self.port, self.connect_timeout)
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/amqp/transport.py", line 187, in _connect
    self.sock.connect(sa)
ConnectionRefusedError: [Errno 111] Connection refused

The above exception was the direct cause of the following exception:

Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<string>", line 1, in <module>
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/celery/local.py", line 182, in __call__
    return self._get_current_object()(*a, **kw)
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/celery/app/base.py", line 947, in send_task
    amqp.send_task_message(P, name, message, **options)
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/celery/app/amqp.py", line 559, in send_task_message
    ret = producer.publish(
          ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/kombu/messaging.py", line 190, in publish
    return _publish(
           ^^^^^^^^^
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/kombu/connection.py", line 558, in _ensured
    return fun(*args, **kwargs)
           ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/kombu/messaging.py", line 200, in _publish
    channel = self.channel
              ^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/kombu/messaging.py", line 224, in _get_channel
    channel = self._channel = channel()
                              ^^^^^^^^^
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/kombu/utils/functional.py", line 34, in __call__
    value = self.__value__ = self.__contract__()
                             ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/kombu/messaging.py", line 240, in <lambda>
    channel = ChannelPromise(lambda: connection.default_channel)
                                     ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/kombu/connection.py", line 960, in default_channel
    self._ensure_connection(**conn_opts)
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/kombu/connection.py", line 460, in _ensure_connection
    with ctx():
         ^^^^^
  File "/users/denolf/.local/wdnbashutils/.pyenv/versions/3.12.11/lib/python3.12/contextlib.py", line 158, in __exit__
    self.gen.throw(value)
  File "/python3.12/site-packages/kombu/connection.py", line 478, in _reraise_as_library_errors
    raise ConnectionError(str(exc)) from exc
kombu.exceptions.OperationalError: [Errno 111] Connection refused
```

The top of the traceback comes from

```python
class ChannelPromise:

    def __init__(self, contract):
        self.__contract__ = contract

    def __call__(self):
        try:
            return self.__value__
        except AttributeError:
            value = self.__value__ = self.__contract__()
            return value

    def __repr__(self):
        try:
            return repr(self.__value__)
        except AttributeError:
            return f'<promise: 0x{id(self.__contract__):x}>'
```
hints_text:
When I change `ChannelPromise` to this (not great because it contains a race-condition)

```python
UNDEFINED = object()

class ChannelPromise:

    def __init__(self, contract):
        self.__contract__ = contract
        self.__value__ = UNDEFINED

    def __call__(self):
        if self.__value__ is UNDEFINED:  # race conditions!!!
            self.__value__ = self.__contract__()
        return self.__value__

    def __repr__(self):
        if self.__value__ is UNDEFINED:
            return f'<promise: 0x{id(self.__contract__):x}>'
        return repr(self.__value__)
```

Another one that works but again with a race condition

```python
class ChannelPromise:
    def __init__(self, contract):
        self._contract = contract
        self._value = None
        self._resolved = False

    def __call__(self):
        if not self._resolved:
            self._value = self._contract()
            # race-condition !!!!!
            self._resolved = True
        return self._value

    def __repr__(self):
        if self._resolved:
            return repr(self._value)
        return f'<promise: 0x{id(self.__contract__):x}>'
```
Ah well, this also solves the traceback issue and does not have a race condition

```python
class ChannelPromise:

    def __init__(self, contract):
        self.__contract__ = contract

    def __call__(self):
        try:
            return self.__value__
        except AttributeError:
            pass
        value = self.__value__ = self.__contract__()
        return value

    def __repr__(self):
        try:
            return repr(self.__value__)
        except AttributeError:
            return f'<promise: 0x{id(self.__contract__):x}>'
```

class ChannelPromise:

        self._contract = contract

Related issue: https://github.com/celery/kombu/issues/1751
Ah well, this also solves the traceback issue and does not have a race condition

```python
class ChannelPromise:

    def __init__(self, contract):
        self.__contract__ = contract

    def __call__(self):
        try:
            return self.__value__
        except AttributeError:
            pass
        value = self.__value__ = self.__contract__()
        return value

    def __repr__(self):
        try:
            return repr(self.__value__)
        except AttributeError:
            return f'<promise: 0x{id(self.__contract__):x}>'
```
